
# CUSTOMER AGREEMENT

**Contract Reference:** COHERON-CA-[YEAR]-[SEQ]
**Version:** 1.0
**Generated by:** CoheronConnect Legal Module · Coheron Tech Private Limited · Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
**Classification:** Confidential

---

## PARTIES

This Customer Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of **[EFFECTIVE_DATE]** ("Effective Date")

**BETWEEN:**

**1. [PROVIDER_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, bearing CIN **[PROVIDER_CIN]**, GSTIN **[PROVIDER_GSTIN]**, PAN **[PROVIDER_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[PROVIDER_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Company");

**AND**

**2. [CLIENT_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, [CLIENT_INCORPORATION_YEAR], bearing CIN **[CLIENT_CIN]**, GSTIN **[CLIENT_GSTIN]**, PAN **[CLIENT_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[CLIENT_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Customer").

---

## RECITALS

(A) Company provides a SaaS platform and associated services ("Services") as described in Schedule 1.

(B) Customer wishes to subscribe to the Services on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement.

(C) This Agreement, together with the Order Form (Schedule 2), governs Customer's access to and use of the Services.

**NOW THEREFORE**, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

**1.1 "Authorised Users"** means individuals authorised by Customer to use the Services, not exceeding the User Limit in the Order Form.

**1.2 "Customer Data"** means all data, content, and information uploaded to or processed through the Services by Customer or Authorised Users.

**1.3 "Documentation"** means all user guides, technical specifications, and help materials made available by Company for the Services.

**1.4 "Free Trial"** means a fourteen (14) day complimentary access period as described in Clause 3, where offered by Company.

**1.5 "Order Form"** means the subscription order form (Schedule 2) specifying the Subscription Tier, User Limit, Fees, and billing details.

**1.6 "Personal Data"** has the meaning assigned under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.

**1.7 "Services"** means Company's SaaS platform and associated support services as described in Schedule 1 and the Order Form.

**1.8 "Subscription Fees"** means the fees payable by Customer as specified in the Order Form.

**1.9 "Subscription Tier"** means the service tier (Starter / Growth / Enterprise or as specified) selected by Customer in the Order Form.

**1.10 "Term"** means the Subscription Term as specified in the Order Form, commencing on the Effective Date.

**1.11 "Dispute"** means any dispute, difference, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

---

## 2. SERVICES

**2.1** Company shall provide Customer with access to the Services during the Term, subject to: (a) payment of Subscription Fees; (b) compliance with this Agreement; and (c) the features and limitations of Customer's Subscription Tier as set out in Schedule 1.

**2.2** Company may update, modify, or enhance the Services from time to time. Company shall provide reasonable notice of material changes that adversely affect Customer's use of the Services.

**2.3** Company shall provide support services as specified in Schedule 3 (Support Terms). Support is available via: email at **[SUPPORT_EMAIL]**, phone at **[SUPPORT_PHONE]**, and portal at **[SUPPORT_URL]**.

---

## 3. FREE TRIAL

**3.1** Where Company offers a Free Trial, Customer shall be entitled to access the Services for a period of **fourteen (14) calendar days** from the date of account creation ("Free Trial Period"), at no charge.

**3.2** During the Free Trial Period: (a) this Agreement applies in full; (b) Customer Data uploaded during the Free Trial may be lost if Customer does not convert to a paid subscription; and (c) Company may terminate the Free Trial at any time without notice.

**3.3** At the end of the Free Trial Period, Customer must subscribe to a paid Subscription Tier to continue using the Services. Continued access after the Free Trial Period constitutes acceptance of the Order Form and the obligation to pay Subscription Fees.

**3.4** THE SERVICES DURING THE FREE TRIAL ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, SLA, OR SUPPORT COMMITMENT.

---

## 4. ACCESS AND USE

**4.1** Customer shall: (a) use the Services only for its internal business operations; (b) ensure Authorised Users comply with this Agreement; (c) maintain the security and confidentiality of Authorised User credentials; (d) promptly notify Company of any unauthorised access or suspected security breach; and (e) not exceed the User Limit specified in the Order Form without purchasing additional seats.

**4.2** Customer shall not, and shall ensure Authorised Users do not: (a) resell, sublicence, or redistribute the Services; (b) reverse engineer or attempt to access the source code of the Services; (c) introduce malicious code or conduct activities that harm the Services or other customers; (d) use the Services to transmit unlawful, defamatory, or infringing content; or (e) use the Services in violation of any applicable law.

---

## 5. CUSTOMER DATA

**5.1 Ownership.** Customer retains all right, title, and interest in and to Customer Data. Company claims no ownership of Customer Data.

**5.2 Licence to Process.** Customer grants Company a limited, non-exclusive licence to access, process, and use Customer Data solely to provide the Services, comply with legal obligations, and improve Service performance.

**5.3 Data Security.** Company shall implement commercially reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect Customer Data against unauthorised access, loss, or disclosure.

**5.4 Data Portability on Exit.** On termination or expiry, Company shall make available a complete export of Customer Data in a standard machine-readable format (JSON/CSV/XML) within **thirty (30) days** of the termination date. After this period, Company may delete Customer Data unless retention is required by applicable law.

---

## 6. SUBSCRIPTION TIERS AND FEES

**6.1 Subscription Tier.** Customer subscribes to the Subscription Tier specified in the Order Form. Tier features and limitations are described in Schedule 1.

**6.2 Fees.** Customer shall pay Subscription Fees as specified in the Order Form.

**6.3 Invoicing.** Company issues tax invoices compliant with GST law at the start of each billing cycle.

**6.4 Payment Terms.** Fees are due within **thirty (30) calendar days** of invoice date, payable in advance.

**6.5 Late Payment.** Overdue amounts accrue interest at **1.5% per month**. Company may suspend access to the Services after fifteen (15) days' notice if Fees remain unpaid.

**6.6 Fee Increases.** Company may revise Subscription Fees for any Renewal Term on not less than **sixty (60) days'** written notice. Continued use after the Renewal Term commencement is acceptance of revised Fees.

**6.7 Upgrades and Downgrades.** Customer may upgrade to a higher Subscription Tier at any time; the incremental Fee is due immediately pro-rated for the remaining Term. Downgrading takes effect from the next Renewal Term.

**6.8 Refund Policy.** All Fees are non-refundable except where: (a) Company terminates this Agreement without cause; or (b) Company fails to meet committed availability levels entitling Customer to service credits under Schedule 3.

---

## 7. TAXES AND GST (M-4)

**7.1 Exclusive of Tax.** All Fees are exclusive of GST and all applicable taxes.

**7.2 GST.** GST at the applicable rate shall be charged additionally per the CGST Act, 2017, IGST Act, 2017, and applicable State GST Act.

**7.3 TDS.** Where Customer must deduct tax at source under the Income Tax Act, 1961, Customer shall: (a) deduct TDS at the applicable rate at time of credit or payment; (b) deposit with tax authorities within prescribed time; (c) issue a valid TDS certificate (Form 16A) to Company within the prescribed period; and (d) file required TDS returns within due dates.

**7.4 Currency.** All Fees are in Indian Rupees (INR), payable by electronic transfer to Company's designated bank account.

---

## 8. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

**8.1 Company IP.** All Intellectual Property Rights in the Services, Software, and Documentation are and remain Company's exclusive property. No ownership rights are transferred to Customer.

**8.2 Feedback.** Any suggestions, ideas, or feedback provided by Customer regarding the Services ("Feedback") may be used by Company without restriction or compensation to Customer.

---

## 9. IP INDEMNIFICATION (M-7)

**9.1** Company shall defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Customer from third-party claims alleging that the Services, as provided and used in accordance with this Agreement, infringe any patent, copyright, trademark, or trade secret recognised under Indian law.

**9.2 Remediation.** If such a claim arises, Company may: (a) procure continued use rights; (b) modify to make non-infringing; (c) replace with non-infringing equivalent; or (d) terminate and refund pre-paid Fees for the unexpired Term.

**9.3 Exclusions.** No obligation for claims arising from: (a) Customer's modification of Services; (b) combination with non-approved products; (c) failure to use available updates; or (d) use not permitted by this Agreement.

**9.4 Process.** Customer shall promptly notify Company, grant sole control of defence (no settlement without Customer's consent), and provide reasonable assistance.

---

## 10. CONFIDENTIALITY

**10.1** Each Party shall hold the other's Confidential Information in strict confidence, use it solely for purposes of this Agreement, and not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent. Confidentiality obligations survive termination for three (3) years.

**10.2** Company's pricing, roadmap, and technical architecture are Company's Confidential Information. Customer Data and business information are Customer's Confidential Information.

---

## 11. DATA PROTECTION — DPDP ACT 2023 (M-3)

**Data Protection and Privacy — DPDP Act 2023.**

- (a) **Applicability.** Applies to the extent either party processes Personal Data in connection with this Agreement.
- (b) **Compliance.** Each party shall comply with all applicable provisions of the DPDP Act, 2023 and rules thereunder.
- (c) **Company as Data Processor.** Company shall: (i) process Personal Data only per Customer's documented lawful instructions; (ii) implement security measures per Section 8 of the DPDP Act; (iii) not engage sub-processors without Customer's written authorisation; (iv) notify Customer within seventy-two (72) hours of any Personal Data Breach; (v) assist with Data Principal rights including access, correction, erasure, and grievance redressal; (vi) delete or return Personal Data on termination; and (vii) permit audits on reasonable notice.
- (d) **Customer as Data Fiduciary.** Customer, as Data Fiduciary, shall obtain valid, informed consent from Data Principals where required under the DPDP Act.
- (e) **Cross-Border Transfers.** Personal Data shall not be transferred outside India except to countries notified under Section 16 of the DPDP Act.
- (f) **Survival.** Obligations survive termination for as long as either party retains Personal Data.

---

## 12. AUDIT RIGHTS (M-6)

**12.1** Company shall maintain records relating to the Services for at least seven (7) years from creation.

**12.2** On not less than fifteen (15) business days' written notice, and no more than once per calendar year, Customer may audit Company's records relating to data processing, security measures, and DPDP Act compliance.

**12.3** Audits are conducted during business hours, without unreasonable disruption, at Customer's expense unless the audit reveals non-compliance of five percent (5%) or more.

---

## 13. WARRANTIES

**13.1 Company Warranties.** Company warrants that: (a) it has full authority to provide the Services; (b) the Services will perform materially in accordance with the Documentation; (c) Company will implement reasonable security measures; and (d) Company holds all licences required to provide the Services.

**13.2 Customer Warranties.** Customer warrants that: (a) it has full authority to enter this Agreement; (b) Customer Data does not infringe any third-party rights; and (c) Customer shall comply with all applicable laws in its use of the Services.

**13.3 Disclaimer.** EXCEPT AS STATED IN CLAUSE 13.1, THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS." COMPANY DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE SERVICES WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED OR ERROR-FREE. ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES ARE EXCLUDED TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE INDIAN LAW.

---

## 14. TERM AND TERMINATION

**14.1 Initial Term.** This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for the period specified in the Order Form.

**14.2 Auto-Renewal.** At the end of each Subscription Term, this Agreement automatically renews for successive periods equal to the initial Subscription Term unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal at least **sixty (60) days** before expiry.

**14.3 Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate immediately on written notice if: (a) the other Party commits a material breach unremedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; or (b) the other Party becomes insolvent.

**14.4 Effects of Termination.** On termination: (a) all Authorised User access ceases; (b) Customer has thirty (30) days to export Customer Data; (c) all outstanding Fees are due; (d) clauses that survive by their nature continue.

---

## 15. FORCE MAJEURE (M-5)

Neither party shall be liable for delay or failure (other than payment obligations) caused by a Force Majeure Event: acts of God, disaster, government-declared pandemic, war, terrorism, civil unrest, acts of government, nationwide strikes, third-party telecom or power failure, or court orders. Economic downturns, revenue loss, or inability to pay are NOT Force Majeure Events. Affected Party notifies in writing within five (5) business days, mitigates, and provides updates. Either party may terminate on thirty (30) days' notice if the event continues for sixty (60) consecutive days.

---

## 16. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (M-8)

**(a)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 16(c), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, GOODWILL, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, HOWEVER ARISING.

**(b)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 16(c), TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED: (i) FEES PAID IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE TWELVE (12) OR MORE MONTHS; OR (ii) TOTAL FEES PAID FROM THE EFFECTIVE DATE WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE LESS THAN TWELVE (12) MONTHS.

**(c)** These limitations do NOT apply to: (i) death or personal injury from negligence; (ii) fraud; (iii) IP indemnification; (iv) DPDP Act obligations including Personal Data Breach liability; (v) wilful misconduct or gross negligence; or (vi) liability that cannot be excluded under Indian law.

**(d)** Each party shall take all reasonable steps to mitigate any loss.

---

## 17. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION AND DIGITAL SIGNATURES (M-2)

Electronic signatures are valid, binding, and enforceable under the Information Technology Act, 2000 (as amended 2008). A digitally signed copy transmitted electronically is treated as an original. Neither party shall contest validity solely on grounds of electronic execution. Digital signatures shall comply with Schedule II of the IT Act, 2000.

---

## 18. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION (M-1)

**(a) Negotiation.** Disputes first referred to senior representatives for good faith resolution within thirty (30) days of written notice.

**(b) Arbitration.** If unresolved, finally resolved by binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (as amended 2021). Sole arbitrator mutually appointed within fifteen (15) days, failing which appointed by the High Court of Karnataka. Seat and venue: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Language: English. Award: final and binding.

**(c) Interim Relief.** Either party may seek interim or injunctive relief from courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

**(d) Exclusive Jurisdiction.** Courts at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India for non-arbitration matters.

**(e) Costs.** Each party bears its own costs unless otherwise ordered.

---

## 19. GENERAL PROVISIONS

**19.1 Entire Agreement.** This Agreement and Schedules constitute the entire agreement between the Parties on its subject matter.

**19.2 Amendments.** No amendment unless in writing signed by authorised representatives of both Parties.

**19.3 Notices.** All notices in writing, by email with read receipt or registered post to the details in Schedule 4.

**19.4 No Waiver.** Failure to exercise any right is not a waiver.

**19.5 Severability.** If any provision is invalid, remaining provisions continue.

**19.6 Assignment.** Customer may not assign without Company's prior written consent. Company may assign to an affiliate or successor.

**19.7 Relationship.** Parties are independent contractors. No partnership, agency, or employment relationship is created.
